articles.title: Heatwave Warning: Heat wave warning issued in 8 states, mercury may reach 41°C in Delhi - informalnewz
articles.full_text:
Sultry heat is expected across several states, including Delhi, UP, and Bihar. In many parts of the country, temperatures have reached up to 40 degrees.
Add informalnewz.com as a Preferred Source
New Delhi: Temperatures are on the rise across India, including in Delhi. Even in mid-April, temperatures in several cities have already reached 40°C. Heatwave-like conditions are being observed prematurely across the Northern Plains, Western regions, and Central India. According to the Meteorological Department, this week may bring discomfort due to heat and humidity, as temperatures continue to climb across the country. In Delhi, the maximum temperature is expected to reach up to 41°C by April 17, while large parts of the country are anticipated to experience hot and humid weather.
Rising Temperatures in These Parts of the Country
According to the Meteorological Department, maximum temperatures in Northwest India are likely to rise by 4 to 6°C by April 18. Parts of Central India—including Madhya Pradesh, Vidarbha, and Chhattisgarh—could witness a temperature rise of 3 to 5°C over the next few days. Gujarat and Maharashtra are also expected to see a gradual increase in temperatures of 2 to 3°C. Hot and humid weather is likely to persist in Odisha, Gujarat, and Coastal Andhra Pradesh for the next seven days. Similar weather conditions are also expected this week in West Bengal, Maharashtra, and certain parts of South India.
In Madhya Pradesh, maximum temperatures are likely to rise gradually by 3–5°C by April 16. Vidarbha and Chhattisgarh could see an increase of 2–4°C by April 17. In Maharashtra, maximum temperatures are expected to rise gradually by 2–3°C by April 17.
Heatwave Warning
The Meteorological Department has issued a heatwave warning for April 14, 15, and 16 covering Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, Bihar, Jharkhand, Gujarat, Andhra Pradesh, Yanam, Rayalaseema, Tamil Nadu, Marathwada, Saurashtra, and Kutch. Yesterday, an increase of 2–4°C in maximum temperatures was observed in East Madhya Pradesh, North Chhattisgarh, Southeast Uttar Pradesh, Interior Odisha, Jharkhand, Gangetic West Bengal, Jammu & Kashmir, South Haryana, and Kutch. The highest maximum temperature in the country—43.1°C—was recorded in Akola (Maharashtra).
Where to Expect Rain
Meanwhile, the Meteorological Department has forecast rain across large parts of Northeast India from April 14 to 17. In the coming days, light to moderate rainfall is likely in Assam and Meghalaya, as well as in other northeastern states such as Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram, and Tripura; this precipitation may be accompanied by thunderstorms, lightning, and strong winds (blowing at 30–50 km/h). Heavy rainfall is also expected in certain parts of Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, and Meghalaya on a few days this week.
